channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration’s dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Investigations into the Texas Lottery grow after courier controversy
item.description: AG Ken Paxton’s office joins two other investigations by state officials into the commission as one service suspends operations due to the agency’s ban.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Trump to subject all contracts, grants through rigorous standards with DOGE executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday, implementing the cost efficiency initiative for DOGE, which looks for government wasteful spending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VA pauses billions in cuts lauded by Musk as lawmakers and veterans decry loss of critical care
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs has temporarily suspended billions of dollars in contract cuts after an uproar that critical veterans health services were harmed, lawmakers and veterans service organizations said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Immigration officials say everyone living in the US illegally must register: What to know
item.description: Federal immigration law has long required that people living illegally in the U.S. register with the government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk and his 'humble tech support' effort get star turn at Trump's Cabinet meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk took a star turn at the first Cabinet meeting of President Donald Trump's new term, holding forth in a black “Make America Great Again” campaign hat on Wednesday about his role as “humble tech support” for the federal government — and laying out dire stakes if his cost-cu
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk tells Cabinet that DOGE email was 'pulse check' for workers, warns US will 'go bankrupt' without action
item.description: Elon Musk said his DOGE productivity email to federal employees was a "pulse check" amid suspicions dead people or "fictional individuals" are on payroll.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Jamieson Greer to be Trump's top trade negotiator as battles loom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate has confirmed Jamieson Greer, a veteran of President Donald Trump's first-term economic battles with China, Mexico and Canada, to be America's top trade negotiator. As U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pushes DOGE, tariffs at first Cabinet meeting
item.description: Musk said the U.S. cannot continue with $2 trillion deficits and huge amounts of interest on the national debt.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump said that he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz officially rules out a run for U.S. Senate
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz had been weighing a Senate run since fellow Democrat Sen. Tina Smith announced earlier this month she wouldn’t run in 2026. Walz expects to decide by early summer if he’ll run for another term as governor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Byron Donalds, backed by Trump, says he's running for Florida governor
item.description: Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds announced Tuesday that he's entering the 2026 race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, who is ineligible to seek another term after eight years in office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass budget resolution, clearing a key early test for Trump agenda
item.description: The GOP framework that passed Tuesday allows the party to move ahead on a sweeping multitrillion plan to address defense, energy, immigration and tax policy.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House to take over press pool amid 'Gulf of America' fight with AP
item.description: The White House said Tuesday that it would take over which outlets are allowed into the press pool covering the president, wrestling such control from the White House Correspondents' Association.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Joy Reid fired from MSNBC amid network shakeup
item.description: Reid is a longtime political commentator who has been vocal on progressive issues and sharply critical of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Jon Stewart cuts hand during fiery monologue on DOGE, prescription drugs
item.description: Jon Stewart is railing against sweeping cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — and giving his hand one of its own in the process — during an impassioned monologue on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UNLV testing cameras to read license plates in aftermath of deadly 2023 attack
item.description: Cameras that can read license plates are part of the security response that followed the Dec. 5, 2023, shooting on the UNLV campus. They are just part of what UNLV is doing in an effort to keep students safer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5
